SQL is fast; NoSQL is slow
SQL uses tables; NoSQL does not support queries

questions.q: What is the purpose of database sharding?
options: Horizontal partitioning of database tables across multiple physical database nodes

questions.q: What is CAP theorem?
options: Consistency, Availability, Partition tolerance trade-off in distributed systems

questions.q: What is a CDN (Content Delivery Network)?
options: Geographically distributed proxy servers caching assets closer to user nodes

questions.q: What is a message broker like RabbitMQ or Kafka used for?
options: Asynchronous service communication, decoupling workloads via queues

questions.q: What is the difference between horizontal and vertical scaling?

Horizontal is cloud; Vertical is local
Horizontal is database; Vertical is API layer

questions.q: What is the purpose of Redis in a web application stack?
options: In-memory key-value database, commonly used for session caching & fast lookups

questions.q: What is Docker?
options: Containerization platform packaging applications with dependencies into isolated environments

questions.q: What is the difference between a Container and a Virtual Machine?

questions.q: What is the purpose of Kubernetes?
options: Container orchestration tool to automate deployments, scaling, and loadbalancing

questions.q: What is the function of an API Gateway?

questions.q: What is rate limiting?
options: Throttling incoming requests to protect server assets from abuse/overloads

questions.q: What is JWT (JSON Web Token)?
options: Self-contained token representing signed user claims, used for stateless authentication

questions.q: What is the difference between symmetric and asymmetric encryption?

Symmetric is secure; Asymmetric is slow

questions.q: What is OAuth 2.0?
options: Authorization framework allowing limited access to third-party assets without exposing passwords
Authentication protocol for enterprise active directories

questions.q: What is the purpose of DNS (Domain Name System)?

questions.q: What is the purpose of an Index in a database table?
options: Speed up data retrieval queries at cost of extra storage and write overhead

questions.q: What is the purpose of Git rebase?
options: Apply commits of a branch on top of another branch to keep linear git history

questions.q: What is the difference between local storage and session storage in browsers?
options: Local storage persists across sessions; Session storage clear when tab closes
Local storage is encrypted; Session storage is not
Local storage size is 1MB; Session storage is 10MB

questions.q: What is a progressive web app (PWA)?
options: A website utilizing service workers to provide offline-first operations and installation
A web app compiled in native Swift

questions.q: What is a service worker in a browser?

questions.q: What is semantic HTML?
options: HTML with clear element tags explaining meaning to browser and screen readers

questions.q: What is the CSS box model?
options: Margin, Border, Padding, Content structure of visual elements

questions.q: What does CSS property box-sizing: border-box do?
options: Includes padding and border in element total width/height calculations

questions.q: What is absolute positioning in CSS?
options: Positioning relative to nearest positioned ancestor

questions.q: What is the purpose of package-lock.json in Node projects?
options: Lock exact dependency versions installed for reproduction consistency

questions.q: What is middleware in Express?
options: Functions executing sequentially during HTTP request-response cycle before route handler

questions.q: What is cross-site scripting (XSS)?
options: Injecting malicious scripts into trusted websites that execute in users browser context

questions.q: What is CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery)?
options: Forcing authenticated users browser to perform unauthorized actions on active sessions

questions.q: What is SQL injection?
options: Injecting malicious SQL statements into user input fields to manipulate backend queries

questions.q: What is a proxy server?
options: Intermediate server forwarding requests between clients and destination servers

questions.q: What is blue-green deployment?
options: Staging app developments on local setups
Release strategy using two identical production environments to minimize downtime

questions.q: What is canary release strategy?
options: Gradually rolling out updates to a small subset of user nodes before global release

questions.q: What is standard RESTful API convention?
options: Stateless operations mapping CRUD to standard HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)

questions.q: Which HTTP status code represents resource successfully created?
Which HTTP status code represents successful request with no content returned?

options: A query language for APIs allowing clients to request precisely the data schema they need

questions.q: What is the benefit of GraphQL over REST?
options: Solves over-fetching and under-fetching by specifying custom response fields
GraphQL is faster than raw sockets

questions.q: What is database replication?
options: Copying database records across multiple servers to improve durability and read throughput

questions.q: What is connection pooling in database clients?
options: Maintaining a cache of reusable database connections to reduce connect overhead

questions.q: What is the purpose of garbage collection in programming languages?
options: Automatic memory management identifying and releasing unused memory allocations

questions.q: What is a memory leak?
options: Memory allocated but not released after use, degrading system performance

questions.q: What is multi-threading?
options: Concurrent execution of multiple threads within a single process sharing heap memory

questions.q: What is a deadlock in concurrent programming?
options: A state where two or more threads are blocked forever, waiting for each other to release locks

questions.q: What is event-driven architecture?
options: System design pattern where flow is determined by events, decoupled via message queues

questions.q: What is the purpose of load testing?
options: Simulating high request volume to check system bottlenecks and break points

questions.q: What is serverless computing (e.g. AWS Lambda)?
options: Cloud computing model where cloud providers dynamically manage server resource allocations

questions.q: What is microservices architecture?
options: Structuring app in huge modular databases
Decomposing applications into small, independently deployable, loosely coupled services

questions.q: What is the role of a reverse proxy like NGINX?
options: Load balancing, SSL termination, and static asset caching in front of backend servers

questions.q: What is SSL/TLS termination?
options: Decrypting encrypted HTTPS traffic at reverse proxy level before forwarding to backend as plain HTTP

questions.q: What is a web hook?
options: HTTP callback trigger sending event notifications to external services automatically

questions.q: What is an index signature in TypeScript?
options: Explicitly typing keys of dynamic object maps

questions.q: What is the virtual DOM reconciler algorithm in React?
options: Diffing algorithm identifying changed nodes to update actual DOM selectively

questions.q: What is static site generation (SSG)?

questions.q: What is incremental static regeneration (ISR)?
options: Updating static pages in background on runtime without rebuilding entire application

questions.q: What is the difference between debouncing and throttling?

Debouncing is for inputs; Throttling is for buttons only

questions.q: What is key-value caching strategy?
options: Caching frequently queried key-value pairs (like queries or sessions) in Redis for O(1) lookups

questions.q: What is write-through caching?
options: Writing data to cache and backing database simultaneously to guarantee consistency
Writing to database, then caching on query

questions.q: What is cache eviction policy like LRU?
options: Removing least recently accessed items when cache threshold is reached

questions.q: What is cache stampede?
options: System overload when multiple threads query database simultaneously for an expired cache key

questions.q: What is the role of index index.css in web systems?

options: Injecting code edits into active browser session without full page refresh

options: Automated pipelines to test, build, and deploy code changes to staging/production

options: Analyzing source code to flag syntax errors, style issues, and bug patterns automatically

questions.q: What is package.json?

questions.q: What is search engine optimization (SEO)?

questions.q: What is the purpose of .env files?

options: A file to ignore git commands logs

A test of compiler syntax correctness

options: Actual path cost from start node to current node n

A heuristic that is equal to g(n)

options: The best already explored value for Minimizer

A mathematical problem defined as a set of objects whose state must satisfy constraints

questions.q: What is the primary representation of knowledge in Expert Systems?

Data-driven reasoning searching forward from known facts to deduce new conclusions

questions.q: What is backward chaining in rule-based AI?
options: Goal-driven reasoning searching backward from target hypothesis to check supporting facts

questions.q: What does ontology represent in knowledge representation?
options: A study of model search paths
A formal representation of categories, properties, and relationships of concepts in a domain

questions.q: What is the purpose of Semantic Networks?
options: Represent knowledge using graph nodes (concepts) and labeled directed edges (relations)

questions.q: What is fuzzy logic?
options: Logic handling approximate truth values between 0 and 1, rather than strict boolean values

questions.q: Which neural network architecture introduced self-attention mechanism?

options: AI subfield focusing on machine understanding and generation of human natural language

questions.q: What is tokenization in NLP?
options: Splitting raw text inputs into smaller units like words or subwords (tokens)

questions.q: What is a Large Language Model (LLM)?
options: A model with huge neural layers predicting next token based on extensive text training
A database system storing dictionary strings

questions.q: Which search algorithm is local, iteratively moving to neighboring state of higher value?

questions.q: What optimization in Hill Climbing allows moves to worse states to escape local maxima?

options: Temperature parameter T, which decreases over schedule time

questions.q: What is crossover in Genetic Algorithms?
options: Combining genetic information of two parent states to generate offspring solutions

questions.q: What is mutation in Genetic Algorithms?
options: Randomly altering candidate properties to maintain genetic diversity in search pool

questions.q: What is reinforcement learning?
options: Learning strategy where agent interacts with environment, receiving rewards or penalties

questions.q: What is Q-learning?
options: Model-free reinforcement learning algorithm learning policy value Q(s, a)

questions.q: In reinforcement learning, what is the exploration-exploitation trade-off?
options: Choosing between trying new actions (exploration) and choosing known high-reward actions (exploitation)

questions.q: What is a Markov Decision Process (MDP)?
options: Mathematical framework for modeling decision making where outcomes are partly random

questions.q: What does the Bellman Equation solve in MDPs?
options: Optimal value function V(s) expressing value of state as current reward + discounted future values

questions.q: What is deep learning?
options: Machine learning using neural networks with many layers (deep architectures)

questions.q: What is an artificial neural network?
options: Computational model inspired by biological brain structure, consisting of connected nodes (neurons)

questions.q: What is the function of an activation function in neural networks?
options: Introduce non-linearity, allowing network to learn complex non-linear patterns

questions.q: Which activation function outputs values in range [0, 1]?

What is ReLU (Rectified Linear Unit) activation function?

options: Algorithm calculating gradients of loss function with respect to weights using chain rule

questions.q: What does gradient descent do in ML training?
options: Optimize model parameters iteratively to minimize loss function

questions.q: What is the learning rate in gradient descent?
options: Step size multiplier taken during parameter updates to minimize loss

questions.q: What is overfitting in machine learning?
options: Running models on low RAM nodes
Model learns training noise instead of general patterns, performing poorly on unseen data

questions.q: What is underfitting in machine learning?
options: Model is too simple to capture underlying patterns of training data

questions.q: What regularization technique randomly drops neural connections during training?
What is a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) primarily used for?
options: Image and grid-like spatial data processing

questions.q: What does a convolution layer do in CNNs?
options: Applies filters to input arrays to extract spatial feature maps

questions.q: What is a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) designed for?
options: Processing sequential and time-series data with memory states

questions.q: What problem do LSTMs (Long Short-Term Memory) resolve in RNNs?
options: Vanishing and exploding gradients in long sequences

questions.q: What is transfer learning?
options: Using a pre-trained model on a new, related task instead of training from scratch

questions.q: What is supervised learning?
options: Training models on labeled data where inputs are mapped to correct outputs

questions.q: What is unsupervised learning?
options: Training models on unlabeled data to find hidden structures/clusters

questions.q: What is k-means clustering?
options: Unsupervised clustering algorithm partitioning data into k distinct clusters based on distance

questions.q: What is the bias-variance tradeoff?
options: Balancing model error from erroneous assumptions (bias) vs error from sensitivity to training noise (variance)

questions.q: What metric evaluates classification accuracy when class distribution is highly imbalanced?
What is the F1-Score?
options: The sum of Precision and Recall
Harmonic mean of Precision and Recall

questions.q: What is an epoch in ML training?
options: One complete pass of entire training dataset through neural network

questions.q: What is a loss function?
options: Function measuring discrepancy between predicted outputs and actual target labels

questions.q: Which loss function is standard for regression tasks?
Which loss function is standard for binary classification tasks?
What optimization algorithm adjusts learning rates dynamically per parameter (e.g. Adam)?
What is pruning in neural networks?
options: Removing non-critical network connections or weights to reduce model size

questions.q: What is self-supervised learning?
options: Form of unsupervised learning where model generates its own labels from input data

questions.q: What is an Autoencoder?
options: Neural network trained to copy its input to its output, commonly used for dimension reduction

questions.q: What is a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)?
options: A model architecture where a Generator and a Discriminator compete to generate realistic data

questions.q: In GANs, what does the Generator do?
options: Create synthetic data attempting to mimic real training distributions

questions.q: In GANs, what does the Discriminator do?
options: Classify inputs as either real training data or fake synthetic data from Generator

questions.q: What is token embeddings in NLP?
options: Mapping tokens/words to continuous high-dimensional vector spaces representing semantic meaning

questions.q: What is the self-attention mechanism?
options: A way to increase model parameters
Calculates dynamic relationships between all tokens in a sequence, weighting their contextual relevance

questions.q: What is prompt engineering?
options: Designing and optimizing text inputs sent to LLMs to elicit target quality responses

questions.q: What is RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)?
options: Training models on new datasets from scratch
Retrieving external document context dynamically to enrich prompts sent to LLMs

questions.q: What is fine-tuning in deep learning?
options: Slightly training a pre-trained model on a smaller, specific dataset to adapt it to a target task

questions.q: Which search algorithm is optimal when heuristics are consistent?
What is the branching factor of a search tree?
options: Number of children/neighbors generated by any node

questions.q: What is a heuristic function in search algorithms?
options: A function checking goal node match
An estimate function calculating remaining path cost to target

questions.q: What is check-pointing in machine learning?
options: Saving active model weights and parameters to disk periodically during training to prevent data loss

questions.q: What is vector database (e.g. Pinecone) used for?
options: Fast similarity search over high-dimensional vector embeddings, ideal for RAG search

questions.q: What is cosine similarity?
options: Metric measuring cosine of angle between two vectors, used to evaluate semantic similarity

questions.q: What is semantic search?
options: Search using semantic meaning represented in vector embeddings rather than keyword match

questions.q: What is classification task in machine learning?
options: Predicting discrete categories/labels for given inputs

questions.q: What is regression task in machine learning?
options: Predicting continuous/numeric values for given inputs

questions.q: What is a Decision Tree?
options: Flowchart-like structure where each internal node represents a test on an attribute, branching to decisions

questions.q: What is a Random Forest?
options: An ensemble of multiple decision trees, averaging predictions for improved accuracy and variance control

questions.q: What is ensemble learning?
options: Combining predictions of multiple base models to generate a more robust prediction

questions.q: What does Support Vector Machine (SVM) do?
options: Finds optimal hyperplane maximizing margin boundary between classes

questions.q: What is the kernel trick in SVMs?
options: A method to speed up writes
Mapping low-dimensional non-linear inputs into higher-dimensional spaces where classes are linear separable

questions.q: What is data leakage in ML?
options: Information from outside the training dataset is used to train the model, creating unrealistic metrics

questions.q: What is principal component analysis (PCA)?
options: Unsupervised dimensionality reduction technique projecting data onto principal component vectors

questions.q: What is standard scaling (z-score normalization)?
options: Rescaling variables to have mean of 0 and standard deviation of 1

questions.q: What is one-hot encoding?
options: Converting categorical variables into binary/dummy vectors of size equal to category count

questions.q: Which evaluation metric evaluates continuous regression models?
What is cross-validation (e.g. k-fold)?
options: Splitting dataset into k subsets, training k times using different subsets as test data to evaluate generalization

questions.q: What does a Confusion Matrix display?
options: Grid layout comparing actual vs predicted classification labels, showing true/false positive/negatives

questions.q: What is the time complexity of predicting outputs using a trained Decision Tree of depth d?
